spec_match.rationale: Trigger drag works and the menu is positioned via anchor positioning, but the script only handles dragging — the script body shows no evidence of `@container anchored(--name)` rules. The fallback-aware layout adaptation is hopefully CSS-only (good if true) but cannot be verified from the snippet.

interactive.rationale: Pointer- and touch-friendly drag of the trigger across the canvas with live anchor repositioning.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb claims 'the container query inside the menu adapts to whichever fallback wins' — that adaptation is supposedly CSS-driven via @container anchored() but the JS doesn't surface which rule is currently active, leaving it visually obvious only when the menu visibly changes shape.

edge_cases.rationale: Four fallback positions (below/above/right/left); drag is bounded to the canvas, so the no-fallback-fits state (which is where the container query is most valuable) is never reachable.

references.rationale: See-also includes https://chromestatus.com/feature/5177580990496768. (Note: spec link points to css-anchor-position-1; the spec for this feature is css-anchor-position-2.)

design_system.rationale: Variables-only colour palette; standard drag-anchor styling.

openQuestions.title: Surface the active @container anchored() rule
openQuestions.detail: Add a small live readout panel next to the canvas showing the currently-applied rule (`@container anchored(--bottom) { ... }`) so visitors can correlate menu position with the spec syntax. Otherwise the demo looks like normal anchor positioning with no obvious payoff from the v143 feature.

openQuestions.title: Fix the spec link to point at css-anchor-position-2
openQuestions.detail: See-also links to https://drafts.csswg.org/css-anchor-position-1/. The actual anchored-container-queries feature is defined in css-anchor-position-2/#anchored-container-queries. The wrong link makes self-heal and reader follow-through worse.

openQuestions.title: Allow the trigger to be dragged outside the stage
openQuestions.detail: If the trigger is bounded to the canvas, the menu always has at least one position-try fallback that fits, so the @container anchored() switch happens silently. Letting the trigger overflow (with the menu fallback chain visibly failing on the last try) would teach the failure mode the spec is built around.

openQuestions.title: Add a 'narrow trigger' option
openQuestions.detail: The menu adapts based on available inline size. A toggle to shrink the trigger element to 24px wide would force the menu into the narrowest fallback and demonstrate the inline-size-aware layout collapse the blurb promises.

summary: Most polished interaction surface of the four concepts (drag + anchor) but the @container anchored() payoff is invisible — surface the active rule and fix the spec link to css-anchor-position-2. The drag boundary also hides the failure-mode demo.
